question_title: Will at least one cabinet-level UK Secretary of State be newly appointed before May 1, 2026?
summary: The question asks whether at least one UK cabinet-level Secretary of State will be newly appointed between March 17, 2026 and May 1, 2026. As of March 25, no Secretary of State change has occurred in the first 8 days of the window. There is active, well-sourced speculation about a major Starmer cabinet reshuffle (targeting Streeting, possibly Reeves), but the critical constraint is timing: most sources indicate the reshuffle is contingent on May 7 local election results, which falls AFTER the May 1 deadline. The Iran/Middle East crisis is further dampening appetite for internal changes. Previous predictions of an imminent reshuffle (from Feb 2026) proved premature. However, there's a non-trivial chance Starmer acts preemptively or that an individual Secretary of State departs independently. The combined probability of a reshuffle before May 1 (~20%) and an individual SoS change (~15%) yields approximately 33%.

factors.description: Active reshuffle speculation from multiple credible sources (Express, Independent, GB News, i Paper) with specific named targets (Streeting, Reeves). Discussion ongoing since at least February 2026.
Timing strongly favors post-May elections - Most sources explicitly tie the reshuffle to AFTER May 7 local election results, which is 6 days past the May 1 deadline.

Iran/Middle East crisis buying Starmer time - Politico reports the war 'has bought him time' by dampening leadership challenge and reshuffle chatter. Multiple COBRA meetings consuming government attention.
Streeting under severe pressure - Published private messages criticizing government, plotting allegations, active discussion of sacking him. But remains in post as of March 25.
Historical base rate - ~200 days since last reshuffle (Sept 2025) with no SoS change despite extreme turbulence. Poisson model gives 19-34% for remaining 37 days.
Broad net across 15+ Secretary of State positions - multiple possible triggering events (resignation, sacking, health issue, scandal) for any one of them.
Previous 'imminent reshuffle' predictions proved wrong - i Paper Feb 16 article said reshuffle 'could come within weeks' after Gorton by-election. 5+ weeks later, no reshuffle.
Some sources suggest pre-election reshuffle - Express implies reshuffle could come before May 7 to prepare for elections, putting late April within resolution window.

reasoning:
**Forecast: 0.33 (33% probability YES)**

**Key reasoning:**

The UK political situation under Starmer is extremely turbulent, with well-sourced reporting from multiple outlets (Express, Independent, GB News, i Paper) about an impending cabinet reshuffle targeting Wes Streeting and possibly Rachel Reeves. However, the critical constraint is **timing**: most sources explicitly tie the reshuffle to the **May 7 local election results**, which falls 6 days **after** the May 1 resolution deadline.

**Factors favoring NO (~67%):**
- The anticipated reshuffle is expected AFTER May 7 elections (past the deadline)
- The Iran/Middle East crisis is consuming government attention and reducing appetite for internal changes
- Previous predictions of imminent reshuffles (Feb 2026) proved premature
- No Secretary of State change has occurred in ~200 days since the Sept 2025 reshuffle

**Factors favoring YES (~33%):**
- Active, credible reshuffle speculation with specific targets named
- Streeting is under severe pressure (leaked messages, plotting allegations)
- 15+ Secretary of State positions = broad net for any change
- Some sources suggest a pre-election reshuffle is possible
- Unexpected events (resignations, scandals) could force a change before May 1

resolution_criteria: This question resolves to yes if GOV.UK shows that at least one cabinet-minister Secretary of State is newly appointed on or after March 17, 2026 and before May 1, 2026, using the official ministers page at https://www.gov.uk/government/ministers. It resolves to no otherwise.
fine_print: The question may also resolve based on  [credible sources](https://www.metaculus.com/faq/#definitions). Count only cabinet-minister Secretary of State appointments, not junior ministers, whips, or parliamentary private secretaries. A reshuffle qualifies if GOV.UK shows a new person holding a Secretary of State role during the resolution window. Candidate Sources: individual GOV.UK minister role pages, for example https://www.gov.uk/government/ministers/secretary-of-state-for-the-home-department
